seo.title: Meet David — Who's to Say Foundation
seo.description: Meet David Richards, the photographer and founder of DJR Photography, and see the creative pathway he is building with Who's to Say Foundation.

listing.title: Meet David
listing.summary: He has a camera, a brand, and a plan. Meet the photographer and founder behind DJR Photography.
listing.label: Participant Story · Vol. 01
listing.imageAlt: David on the sideline at a night football game holding his Nikon camera

hero.eyebrow: A Who's to Say story · Vol. 01
hero.title: Meet David. He has a camera, a brand, and a plan.
hero.lead: He's not someone who takes pictures sometimes. He's a professional photographer. He has clients in mind. He has a slogan. He designed his own logo. We sat down and listened, and work together as peers.
hero.imageAlt: David on the sideline at a night football game, hooded in a blue jacket, holding his Nikon camera
hero.badge: Dream big. — David, on his slogan

sections.heading: He designed it himself. That matters.
paragraphs: David Richards is a photographer. Not someone who takes pictures sometimes — a photographer. He has clients in mind. He has a brand. He has dreams that are bigger than most people take the time to sit down and actually listen to.
That's exactly what Who's to Say Foundation did. We sat down. We listened. He handed us a card.
sections.imageAlt: DJR — David J. Richards Photography logo
sections.caption: David's own brand. Designed by David.

sections.heading: His camera sees things differently.
paragraphs: David doesn't just point and shoot. He organizes. He archives. He has 740 photos backed up, sorted, protected — because he knows his work has value and he treats it that way.
When we first worked together on his photo library, he navigated his albums with focus and intention. He knew exactly what he wanted. He knew what he was building.
That kind of clarity is not something you teach. That's someone who already knows who they are.
sections.imageAlt: David lining up a shot on the sideline of a sports field with a tripod-mounted telephoto lens
sections.caption: On location · Fall 2025

sections.role: He came up with it. We wrote it down.

sections.heading: What we're building together.
sections.lead: At Who's to Say, we don't hand people a script. We hand them a camera, a design tool, and a platform, and let them tell us what they see. This is the path we're building for David, and for every young adult who walks in next.

items.title: Launch · A real photography business
items.body: Branding, pricing, a website, and an LLC if he wants one. Clients who pay him for his work, not for showing up.

items.title: Make · Merchandise he designs
items.body: DJR Photography clothing, accessories, and prints. David sets the direction; we provide the tools and the storefront.

items.title: Grow · A creative identity, on his terms
items.body: Studio access, professional gear, and one-on-one coaching. Not a program. A pathway.

sections.heading: At the end of a session he looked up and said he was excited to learn something new. That sentence is why Who's to Say exists.
sections.imageAlt: David at the track with a friend, behind a Nikon camera, laughing on a sunny afternoon

sections.heading: See the brand and body of work David is building.
sections.body: DJR Photography remains David's participant-owned portfolio, separate from this Foundation story.

sections.heading: David's dream doesn't need you to fix anything.
paragraphs: It needs resources. Tools. Time. A studio. A mentor sitting across the table who says: yes, that idea is good, let's build it. Your gift makes that table available to more young adults like David.

sections.heading: Who's to say he can't? He already is.
sections.body: Your gift to Who's to Say Foundation funds the studio, the mentor, and the table where dreams like David's get built. Not as a program. As a pathway.
